title: Conformal Prediction, by Hand
tagline: Prediction intervals with a coverage guarantee — assumption-free, and one quantile away
summary: Pick any model. Hold out a calibration set. Compute the residuals. Take their (1−α) quantile. The resulting interval contains the truth with probability at least (1−α) — distribution-free, model-free. Live regression and classification demos confirm the coverage on synthetic data, then we walk through adaptive (CQR) and time-series-friendly (ACI) variants.
highlights: Live split-conformal regression on a synthetic 1-D dataset; toggle the calibration size and target coverage.
Coverage diagnostic: empirical-coverage bar that should hover at 1−α regardless of the underlying model.
CQR (conformalised quantile regression) — adaptive widths with the same coverage guarantee.
Time-series ACI (adaptive conformal inference) for non-exchangeable data.
